world.title: Sudan: Second Phase of Currency Exchange to Begin in Khartoum and Al-Gezira On April 15
world.description: Khartoum, April 12, 2026 (SUNA) - The Central Bank of Sudan (CBOS) has announced the launch of the second phase of its currency exchange process in Khartoum and Al-Gezira on April 15, running until May 15, alongside the completion of exchange operations in localities of White Nile not covered in the first phase.

world.title: Tanzania: Z'bar Bets On Youth Innovation to Drive Digital Economic Growth
world.description: ZANZIBAR government has stressed its commitment to nurturing youth innovation and technology as a pathway to job creation, digital economic growth and global competitiveness.

world.title: Trump refuses to apologise after clash with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
world.description: Despite widespread criticism including Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni's rare rebuke after the US president lambasted the US-born pontiff for being "weak on crime," Trump said no to an apology to the Catholic Church's Holy Father while reiterating his claims.

world.title: Open Call: Building Communities Grant Program (Canada)
world.description: Deadline: 01-May-2026 The Building Communities Grant Program provides up to $50,000 in capital funding plus fundraising support to help Calgary communities design and build inclusive outdoor spaces. It focuses on improving access to nature, play, and public gathering areas while promoting accessibility, safety, and environmental sustainability. world.title: Second man charged over Finbar Sullivan stabbing in Primrose Hill
world.description: An 18-year-old is accused of grievous bodily harm with intent, following the death of Finbar Sullivan.

world.title: Uganda: Museveni Hails Gen Saleh's Sacrifice, Patriotism in Liberation Struggle
world.description: President Museveni has hailed Caleb Akandwanaho, famously known as Gen. Salim Saleh, for sacrificing his youthful days to join the liberation struggle, describing him as a symbol of patriotism and a role model for young Ugandans.

business.title: AI Skills Africa leads AI Skills Fest 2026 at Google AI Centre, empowering Ghana’s workforce for the future
business.description: AI Skills Africa, in partnership with FestMan, has successfully hosted AI Skills Fest 2026 at the Google AI Centre in Accra, Ghana, bringing together a diverse group of professionals, students, and innovators for a transformative, hands-on learning experience. Held under the theme "Explore, Build and Innovate with AI," the event attracted participants from across academia and industry, including professionals in human resources, accounting, finance, procurement, and technology, as well as students from Accra Technical University, the University of Cape Coast, the University of Ghana, and Ghana Telecom University.

science.title: The moon's oldest and darkest craters could be hiding the most water ice
science.description: New research shows that the craters that have been in permanent shadow the longest close to the lunar south pole are more likely to contain the most water-ice.

ai.title: How Anthropic’s Mythos AI has exposed India to a cybersecurity worry with sovereignty implications

ai.title: 8 Probiotic-Loaded Breakfast Foods That Indians Love
ai.description: Indian cuisine is loved all over the world for its curries, spices, textures, and more. But not many talk about the probiotic angle. Fermented foods or curd-based dishes are excellent for the gut, as they are sources of probiotics, and the plus side is that Indians love them in summer for their cooling effects.
